name: FM 21-76 US Army Survival Manual (Internet Archive, full PDF)
note: Public domain US Army survival manual; covers all environmental survival scenarios including cold, desert, water, jungle, mountain. Foundational SERE reference.

name: FM 21-76 Direct PDF Download (Internet Archive)
note: Direct-link PDF of the complete FM 21-76. Save for offline use.

name: Ready.gov: Build a Kit
note: FEMA's primary civilian emergency supply kit page; links to printable checklist PDF

name: FEMA Ready.gov — Emergency Supply List (Printable PDF)
note: Printable FEMA emergency supply list. Suitable for inclusion in a go-bag.

name: Red Cross — 11 Survival Skills That Could Save Your Life
note: American Red Cross overview of core survival skills for civilians. Accessible introduction.

name: Wilderness First Aid Training — American Red Cross
note: Red Cross WFA certification courses; 2-year certification for outdoor and remote first aid

name: NOLS Wilderness Medicine — Course Catalog
note: NOLS offers WFA, WFR, and WEMT certifications. WFR is the standard credential for backcountry guides and military personnel in non-TCCC roles.

name: Stop the Bleed — National Campaign Website
domain: Military, Tactical, Survival, and Medical Acronyms — Field Reference Glossary
note: Find local Stop the Bleed courses; teaches three steps: apply pressure, pack the wound, apply tourniquet; no prior training required

name: NOAA PLB Registration (Beacon Registration)
note: All PLBs used in the US must be registered here. Registration is free and ensures SAR responders receive owner/deployment data when beacon activates.

name: Wilderness-Survival.net — Water Procurement Chapter
note: Detailed chapter-level treatment of water finding, solar stills, and purification. Mirrors FM 21-76 Chapter 6 structure.

name: Wilderness-Survival.net — Signaling Techniques
note: Detailed overview of ground-to-air signals, mirror use, signal fires, and SOS patterns from the FM 21-76 signaling chapter.

name: Art of Manliness — Wilderness Distress Signals
note: Well-illustrated civilian reference covering whistle, mirror, fire, and ground-to-air signaling conventions.

name: OFFGRID — International Ground-to-Air Signaling Code
note: Visual reference for the international ground-to-air symbol system used to communicate with rescue aircraft.

name: Montana Knife Company / John Barklow — Rule of Threes
note: Authored by a military survival instructor. Solid tactical application of the Rule of Threes framework.

name: Montana Knife Company / John Barklow — Improvised Shelter Building
note: Practical construction details for debris huts, lean-tos, and other improvised shelters from a military survival background.

name: Paul Kirtley — STOP: First Response to Being Lost
note: Detailed application of the STOP method from a UK bushcraft instructor. Covers psychology of panic and decision-making under stress.

name: Georgia Bushcraft — 4 Common Navigation Myths That Could Get You Lost
note: Debunks the moss-on-trees myth and other unreliable navigation folklore. Important for preventing overconfidence in primitive navigation.

name: USGS National Map Downloader — Free Topo Maps
note: Free download of current and historical USGS topographic maps (GeoPDF, GeoTIFF) for any US location; public domain

name: FM 21-76-1 — Survival, Evasion, and Recovery (FAS.org)
note: Companion to FM 21-76 covering SERE principles; hosted at Federation of American Scientists

name: FM 4-25.11 First Aid (Internet Archive, full text)
note: US Army/Navy/Air Force first aid manual (2002); covers hypothermia, frostbite, heat injuries, bites, and field first aid procedures.

name: CDC: How to Make Water Safe in an Emergency
note: CDC guidance on boiling, bleach treatment, and water purification tablets

name: EPA: Emergency Disinfection of Drinking Water
note: EPA bleach dosage tables by container size and bleach concentration; calcium hypochlorite instructions; iodine dosing.

name: Ready.gov: Water Storage and Purification
note: Official guidance on how much to store, container selection, and purification methods

name: FEMA: Emergency Water Sources FAQ
note: FEMA answers on sourcing water in disasters, improvised sources, and what NOT to drink.

name: Princeton Outdoor Action: Guide to Water Purification
note: Detailed backcountry water purification guide; organism size/filter pore tables; iodine and chlorine dioxide dosing; Giardia and Cryptosporidium treatment.

name: HPRC Warfighter Nutrition Guide Chapter 13: Combat Rations
note: Human Performance Resource Center guide to all US military ration types, calorie specs, protein/fat/carb breakdowns, and proper use.

name: US Army Combat Rations Operational Guide (Quartermaster, army.mil)
note: Practical guide to when to use each ration type (MRE, UGR, FSR) and operational calorie planning.

name: AR 40-25: Nutrition and Menu Standards for Human Performance Optimization
note: Joint service regulation governing all military ration nutritional standards.

name: USDA FSIS: Shelf-Stable Food Safety
note: USDA guidance on canned goods, shelf-stable foods, date codes, and food safety for stored emergency supplies.

name: USU Extension: Food Storage Research Hub
note: Utah State University research-backed shelf-life data for white rice, wheat, beans, honey, canned goods, and freeze-dried foods with packaging methods.

name: USU Extension: Storing White Rice
note: Detailed shelf-life data including 30-year BYU study on polished rice quality retention under various storage conditions.

name: CDC: Water Treatment Options for Hiking, Camping, and Traveling
note: CDC comparison of boiling, chemical, filter, and UV methods; organism categories addressed by each method.

name: Cleveland Clinic: Foraging 101 — What to Eat and Avoid
note: Medical-grade foraging safety overview; dangerous look-alikes; poisoning symptoms and when to call 911.

name: Mississippi State University Extension: Introduction to Wild Edible Plants
note: Peer-reviewed introduction to foraging; regional plant identification; safety and sustainability guidelines.

name: PubMed: Ad libitum water consumption prevents exertional hyponatremia — Army 40-km march study
note: Primary research on military hyponatremia vs. dehydration risk during sustained operations; supports programmed drinking limits.

name: Stop the Bleed — Official Training Program (ACS)
note: Official Stop the Bleed course locator; over 5 million trained since 2015; free and paid classes available nationwide

name: DoD Stop the Bleed — Department of Defense Program
note: DoD-specific hemorrhage control training resources; public access

name: NAEMT — TCCC Courses for Civilians
note: Accredited TCCC-CMC and TCCC-CLS courses endorsed by the Joint Trauma System and American College of Surgeons.

name: NAEMT TECC Course Information
note: Tactical Emergency Casualty Care course for civilian/law enforcement first responders

name: C-TECC Guidelines (Free PDF)
note: Official 2024 TECC guidelines for first responders; free public download from the Committee for Tactical Emergency Casualty Care

name: C-TECC Official Website
note: Committee for Tactical Emergency Casualty Care; publishes guidelines, FAQs, and course information

name: Joint Trauma System Clinical Practice Guidelines
note: Free public PDFs of all JTS CPGs covering hemorrhage, hypothermia, TBI, airway, and more; authoritative US military trauma standards

name: TCCC Handbook Version 5 — US Army (army.mil)
type: Official US Army TCCC handbook (free PDF)

note: Distro-A (public release) version of the TCCC handbook covering all three phases of care

note: Official US Army First Aid training circular; publicly available via Army training library; covers basic self/buddy aid

note: Predecessor to TC 4-02.1; free streaming and download via Internet Archive; comprehensive illustrated self/buddy first aid

name: TCCC Guidelines — CoTCCC / Joint Trauma System (Allogy hosted)
domain: Military, Tactical, Survival, and Medical Acronyms — Field Reference Glossary
note: Current Committee on Tactical Combat Casualty Care guidelines including 2026 updates on tourniquet reassessment, airway management, and iTClamp

name: NAEMT TCCC Instructor Guide — Massive Hemorrhage (Free PDF)
note: NAEMT open-access instructor guide for TFC hemorrhage module; covers tourniquet, wound packing, and hemostatic agents in detail

name: NAEMT TCCC Instructor Guide — Airway (Free PDF)
note: NAEMT open-access instructor guide for airway management; NPA insertion, surgical airway, recovery position

name: NAEMT TCCC Instructor Guide — Respiration/Breathing (Free PDF)
note: NAEMT open-access instructor guide covering chest seals, needle decompression, and tension pneumothorax

name: JTS Hypothermia Prevention and Treatment CPG
note: 2023 Joint Trauma System clinical practice guideline for hypothermia prevention and treatment in trauma; free public PDF

name: NOLS Wilderness First Responder Course
note: Gold standard WFR certification (70-80 hours); covers patient assessment, trauma, environmental emergencies, and prolonged care in austere settings

name: Red Cross First Aid and CPR Training
note: American Red Cross first aid/CPR/AED courses; foundational civilian training; available in-person and online nationwide

name: EMS Junctional Hemorrhage Control — StatPearls (NCBI/NIH)
note: Free peer-reviewed StatPearls article on junctional hemorrhage control techniques; covers XStat, wound packing, junctional tourniquets

name: Crisis Medicine — MARCH/MATCH/MARCH-PAWS Analysis
note: Analysis of 2025 CoTCCC discussions on updating the MARCH mnemonic for the blood-product resuscitation era; useful background on protocol evolution

name: ProlongedFieldCare.org — RAVINES Mnemonic for PFC
note: Practical guide to the RAVINES framework for prolonged field care beyond initial TCCC; telemedicine, resuscitation, ventilation priorities

name: ATP 3-21.18 Foot Marches (April 2022) — Federation of American Scientists
note: Primary Army governing publication for foot marches, load categories, and ruck standards. Public release; distribution unlimited.

name: ATP 3-21.18 Foot Marches (July 2025) — TRAIN.army.mil
note: Most current edition of the foot marches ATP as of mid-2025.

name: FM 7-21.13 / TC 7-21.13 The Soldier's Guide — FAS.org
note: Foundational soldier reference covering load-bearing, field craft, and individual soldier standards. Approved for public release.

name: Soldier Load: The Art and Science of Fighting Light — Army University Press (Infantry Magazine Fall 2024)
note: Current analysis of soldier load management challenges and doctrine from the Infantry School.

name: ATP 3-21.8 Combat Loads Supplement — Fort Benning / Army.mil
note: Defines fighting load vs. approach march load in the context of small-unit infantry operations.

name: Foot Blisters Take the Fun Out of Marching — U.S. Army Official Article
note: Official Army guidance on blister prevention, sock selection, and field treatment.

name: Military Ruck Standards by Branch (2026) — The Ruck Calculator
note: Comprehensive summary of distance, weight, and time standards across Army, Rangers, SFAS, Air Assault, Marine Corps, and Navy SEAL programs.

name: How to Train for Army Ruck Marches — GORUCK
note: Practical training guidance for the 12-mile/35-lb/3-hour standard, from a company founded by Special Forces veterans.

name: The 12-Mile Ruck: Standards, Time, and Prep — GORUCK
note: Detailed breakdown of the 12-mile ruck march benchmark event, prep program, and gear requirements.

name: Get Started Rucking: Complete Beginner Guide — GORUCK
note: Entry-level rucking overview: starting weight, pack selection, frequency, and health benefits.

name: Rucking Injury Prevention Guide: 10 Common Injuries — RuckingBasics.com
note: Covers all 10 major rucking injuries with causes, prevention strategies, and return-to-training guidance.

name: Rucking Recommendations: Lower Back and Knee Pain — O2X Human Performance
note: Evidence-based guidance on form, load distribution, and strength training from a military human performance organization.

name: How Heavy Should Your Ruck Be? — Team Red, White and Blue
note: Practical load-selection guide for veterans and civilians; discusses the 1/3 bodyweight rule and beginner scaling.

name: 3 Tips for Packing a Ruck — Team Red, White and Blue
note: Load distribution and pack organization guidance for civilian ruckers.

name: The Secret to a Blister-Free Foot March — The Military Leader
note: Detailed sock layering, boot prep, and hotspot management guide targeting military and civilian marchers.

name: 12-Week Rucking Training Plan — The Ruck Calculator
note: Structured 12-week progressive training plan from beginner to event-ready, with weekly mileage and load progressions.

name: Free Rucking Workout Plans — GORUCK
note: Free beginner and advanced ruck training plans from GORUCK, including weekly structure and strength supplements.

name: Comprehensive Guide to Rucking for Special Operations — SOF Prep Coach
note: SFAS and Ranger School preparation framework including recommended ruck standards and periodized training approach.

note: Direct PDF of the current US Army Training Circular (Nov 2013); public release; the authoritative reference for all Army land navigation doctrine

note: The authoritative Army land navigation manual covering MGRS, compass, terrain association, and map reading

name: NOAA Magnetic Declination Calculator — NCEI/NGDC
note: Authoritative government tool for current magnetic declination at any location worldwide; uses World Magnetic Model (WMM)

name: CalTopo — Backcountry Mapping and Route Planning
note: Free/freemium web tool for pre-mission mapping; supports USGS topos, MGRS grid overlays, slope angle shading, and printable custom maps

note: Mobile GPS app (iOS/Android) with offline topo maps; free tier available; integrates with CalTopo for pre-planned routes

name: OsmAnd — Offline Maps and Navigation
note: Full-featured offline navigation app for Android and iOS using OSM data. F-Droid tracker-free build available.

note: Free online converter between MGRS, lat/long, and UTM; interactive map display; no signup required

note: UTM system explained: zones, eastings, northings, relationship to MGRS; good primer before learning MGRS

note: Civilian-oriented guide to declination: what it is, east vs west, how to set adjustable compasses, conversion rules

note: Practical guide with typical pace count values (62-75 per 100m), ranger bead construction and use, terrain adjustment factors

note: Well-maintained encyclopedia article on MGRS; covers GZD structure, 100k squares, precision levels, relationship to UTM

note: Hosted download of the superseded FM 3-25.26 for historical reference; same core content as TC 3-25.26 for most land nav topics

note: Accessible civilian introduction to topo map reading: contour lines, index contours, valley vs ridge signatures, scale, and using digital topos

name: TC 4-02.1 First Aid (2016) — Army Pubs official PDF
note: Updated Army first aid doctrine including environmental injuries. Superseded by ATP 4-02.11 (2026) but widely referenced.

name: FM 31-70 Basic Cold Weather Survival Manual (Internet Archive)
note: Dedicated cold weather survival manual; covers shelter, clothing, travel, frostbite, hypothermia in detail.

name: NOAA / NWS Wind Chill Chart and Calculator
note: Official National Weather Service wind chill formula and frostbite risk table; includes frostbite time-to-injury thresholds at various temperature/wind combinations.

name: Army Public Health — Cold Weather Casualties and Injuries
note: US Army Public Health Command reference page on cold injury categories, prevention, and treatment for military and civilian audiences.

name: Army Heat Illness Prevention and Sun Safety
note: Defense Health Agency resource covering WBGT, heat categories, work/rest tables, and heat injury prevention for military personnel.

name: FEMA / Ready.gov — Extreme Heat
note: FEMA public preparedness guide for extreme heat events; practical home and outdoor guidance for general public.

name: CDC Yellow Book — High Elevation Travel and Altitude Illness
note: CDC traveler's health reference covering AMS, HACE, HAPE, acetazolamide, and descent protocols. Free online access.

name: WildsafeOrg — Altitude Illnesses 101 (AMS, HACE, HAPE)
note: Clear lay-audience summary of all three altitude illness types with symptoms, treatment, and Gamow bag guidance.

name: Wilderness Medical Society — Frostbite Clinical Practice Guidelines 2024
note: Current evidence-based WMS guidelines on frostbite prevention, classification, field rewarming, and hospital treatment. Abstract free; full text may require access.

name: DTIC — Sustaining Health and Performance in Cold (ADA395745, full PDF)
note: US DoD technical report on cold weather physiology, clothing, nutrition, and performance. Freely available via DTIC.

name: NOLS Wilderness Medicine — Resources and Free Study Materials
note: NOLS provides free WFR/WFA practice tests, handbook excerpts, scope-of-practice charts, and case studies. Leading wilderness medicine training organization.

name: Red Cross — Exertional Heat Stroke First Aid
note: American Red Cross public first aid guidance for heat stroke recognition and cooling treatment; free online access.

name: Navy/Marine Corps Public Health — Prevention and Treatment of Heat and Cold Stress Injuries
note: Comprehensive Navy/USMC technical manual covering heat and cold stress physiology, prevention, and treatment protocols.

name: NIH/NCBI StatPearls — Acute Mountain Sickness
note: Free full-text evidence-based review of AMS etiology, pathophysiology, diagnosis, and management; continuously updated.

name: TRADOC Regulation TR350-29 — Prevention of Heat and Cold Casualties (PDF)
note: US Army TRADOC regulation governing heat and cold casualty prevention in training; includes work/rest tables and WBGT implementation.

name: Minnesota DNR — Cold Water Dangers
note: Clear public resource on cold water immersion survival time, cold shock, H.E.L.P. position, and PFD importance; applicable to any cold water environment.
